rulings.Case Title: ANH L. TROUNG VS. RUBY TANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Renewed Motion And Motion To Shield Or Redact Defendant Ruby Tangs Address From Court Records Pursuant To Crc 2.550
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, October 21, 2024, Line 2, DEFENDANT RUBY TANG's Renewed Motion To Shield Or Redact Defendant Ruby Tangs Address From Court Records Pursuant To CRC 2.550. Defendant's motion to redact her address from court records is granted. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, October 21, 2024, Line 3, PLAINTIFF GOOD HOPE URBANITE PREMIUM LINKED FUND, LLC's Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint. Plaintiff's motion for leave to file second amended complaint is granted. rulings.Case Title: CAT'S TAIL, LLC, VS. KATHERINE M. PAPAGROGRE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Relief From Default; **First Appearance Fees Paid**
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, October 21, 2024, Line 4, 2- CROSS-DEFENDANTS MICHAEL CHRISTL, and CNC INVESTMENTS, INC.'s Motion For Relief From Default. Cross-defendants Michael Christl and CNC Investments, Inc.'s motion to vacate the defaults entered on July 19, 2024, is granted. Cross-defendants are entitled to discretionary relief per CCP 473(b). "The law favors judgments on the merits, not procedural missteps. Our Supreme Court has repeatedly reminded us that in this area doubts must be resolved in favor of relief []." (Lasalle v. Vogel (2019) 36 Cal.App.5th 127, 134 (italics in original).) Cross-defendants filed this motion within a reasonable time. The defaults were entered on July 19, 2024, and cross-defendants filed this motion on September 5, 2024. Cross-defendants demonstrate mistake, inadvertence and/or excusable neglect. Cross-defendants believed that the lender was going to defend them and when they learned that was not the case, they immediately retained counsel and attempted to file a petition to compel arbitration. (Christl Decl., pars. 4-6; Wilkinson Decl., Ex. I (attached to reply).) Lastly, the court finds that the petition to compel arbitration qualifies as a proposed pleading under CCP 473(b). CCP 473 is a remedial statute and the court liberally construes it. A petition to compel arbitration may be filed in lieu of an answer. (CCP 1281.7.) The defaults are vacated. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: LAKSHMI PALADUGU VS. COMBINED INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, October 21, 2024, Line 13, DEFENDANT BRUCE CHEN'S MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE. Defendant Bruce Yu Chen's "motion for transfer of venue" is denied. The complaint pleads that Chen sold plaintiff a Combined Insurance Company of America life insurance policy with a long-term-care rider. A coverage dispute arose. Combined is a foreign corporation with no principal place of business in California, so it can be sued in any California county. (Jalali Dec. Ex. 1; Easton v. Sup. Ct. (1970) 12 Cal.App.3d 243, 246-47.) Moreover, as Chen states, "so long as the plaintiff chooses a venue that is proper as to one defendant [here San Francisco], the entire case may be tried there." (Memo. 4:12-13, citing Monogram Co. v. Kingsley (1951) 38 Cal.2d 28, 29.) Chen moves to transfer under CCP 397 "on the ground that" Los Angeles County "is the proper venue." (Memo. 1:21-27.) However, as shown above, San Francisco is a proper venue as well; no one "the proper venue" exists for this case. (See Easton, 12 Cal.App.3d at 247 [movant's burden "of showing the action was not begun in a proper county"].) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: QUEENS SF, LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY PARTNERSHIP ET AL VS. NICOLE DOBARRO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Waiver Of Bond Requirement Under Cal. Labor Code Section 98.2; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Monday, October 21, 2024, Line 14, APPELLANTS QUEENS SF, LLC, and EDWARD KIM's Motion For Waiver Of Bond Requirement Under Cal. Labor Code Section 98.2. Appellants Queens SF, LLC and Edward Kim's motion for wavier of bond requirement is denied as moot because the underlying appeal is untimely. Queens SF and Kim appeal from a Labor Commissioner decision awarding former employee Nicole Dobarro $74,419.82 for Labor Code violations. Labor Code section 98.2(a) provides, "[w]ithin 10 days after service of notice of an order, decision, or award the parties may seek review by filing an appeal to the superior court." CCP section 1013 extends the period to appeal by five calendar days, if the papers were mailed from within California. Here, the Labor Commissioner order was served on August 14, 2024, by mail from San Bernardino, California. So, appellants' last day to appeal was August 29, 2024. Appellants' appeal was filed on August 30, 2024, one day too late. (See 8/30/24 ROA entry "Notice of Appeal.") The appeal is untimely, and Labor Code 98.2(d) leaves no discretion for the court to rule otherwise. (See subd. (d) "[i]f no notice of appeal is filed within the period set forth in subdivision (a), the order, decision, or award shall, in the absence of fraud, be deemed the final order.") For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
